title: Is the US-Israeli attack on Iran legal?
author: Guardian News
fullText: The United States and its partners have launched Operation Epic Fury. [music] >> What is really striking about this is it is done without any nod towards international law, without any real consultation with either the US Congress or the public for what looks like a a major war. Saturday morning about 9 or 10 uh local time in Tehran, the US and Israel struck Iran all over the country. It was a decapitation strike aimed at the leadership of [music] the military. The Supreme Leader Ali Ham. As soon as this attack began, a long planned Iranian response went into action. >> Most of those missiles have been intercepted by either the the US, Israel, or the host countries of US bases in in the region. But some have got through. Under the US Constitution, it is Congress that is supposed to uh decide whether the the nation should go to war or peace. Now that has [music] been ignored or sidestepped by previous presidents. But what is striking about this is its closest uh comparison is the 2003 invasion of Iraq. It's about regime change of a a major country, a major adversary. Uh and in that case, the Bush administration did get congressional authority. Donald Trump hasn't really bothered to make a case. Congress [music] is being treated as basically redundant. This is really the the first major war of Trump's border peace era and the second time in barely 2 months that he's removed the head of state of a troublesome country. It confirms some of the worst fears about what this border [music] peace would be about. Uh and that is a way around having to deal with the UN and the UN charter. And this is what we're seeing his attempt to rewrite the sort of rule book of international legal behavior as one where he alone makes the rules. The kind of collapse of that so-called rule-based order means now that anything is possible and all of our assumptions have been swept aside.
